title: kittui-wm v2: backend-agnostic compositor (local/remote/Mac/X11 windows as homogeneous kittui chrome)
description:
Generalise kittui-wm so any window — local X11, remote X11 over SSH, native macOS via Quartz capture, XQuartz-hosted X11, future Wayland, future Tendril — appears in the user's terminal as the same kittui chrome surface. The compositor multiplexes multiple XServer backends behind one operator UI, with globally-unique window ids, per-window WindowMode (Floating or Tiled), per-window chrome theme, and a single hit-test/route_pointer/route_key pipeline. v2 must let the operator mix Mac windows (Quartz CGWindowList capture) with XQuartz X11 windows in the same session, all rendered with locally-composed kittui chrome.

2026-05-22 burn-down note: implementation child beads for native PTY default, native browser, socket env/protocol, XQuartz wrapper, picker overlay, relative placement, animation grammar, docs, and markdown/UI initial stack landed and were closed. Remaining SSH latency proof is linked as dependent/future work rather than blocking this implementation burn-down.

title: Set q=2 quiet flag on all kitty control commands

Wire a per-Runtime quiet mode (default q=2) and surface it via TerminalInfo / Runtime builder; preserve a verbose path for debugging.

Need to prepend the placement with \x1b[r;cH when footprint.x/y are non-zero (or expose a separate api). Parent: bd-42d7c1.

title: Implement Transport::File and Transport::Memory upload paths
description:
Transport enum currently advertises File and Memory but encode_chunked always emits a=t,f=100 streaming base64. Per spec t= selects the transmission medium (d direct, f file, t temp file, s shared memory) and the payload becomes a path or shm name instead of pixel bytes. Implement real File (write PNG to a temp path, send t=f with path) and Memory (POSIX shm or anonymous mmap, send t=s) transports, with cleanup, error handling, and fallbacks. Add tests and a CLI flag to select transport.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

The 'q to quit' message in the footer changes to 'Ctrl-C Ctrl-C Ctrl-C to quit' once any backend is hosting an app that might consume Ctrl-C. Parent epic: bd-031e54

title: Complete kitty graphics protocol conformance + ratakittui coverage
description: Operator-confirmed gap: kittui was supposed to be a careful extraction of the Cacophony TUI graphics lib against https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol/ but the showcase reproduces ENOENT image-not-found responses in Ghostty and produces only placeholder character grids. Audit shows multiple spec deviations: (1) unicode placeholders emit bare U+10EEEE with foreground color only, missing the (row, column, msb-of-image-id) combining diacritics that the spec requires for cell-to-pixel anchoring; (2) the q= quiet field is never set so kitty prints diagnostic responses inline; (3) declared Transport::File and Transport::Memory never adjust t= and still base64-stream via a=t; (4) animation upload uses a=t with r= instead of a=t for first frame then a=f for subsequent frames and the animation control verb shape is wrong; (5) placement command lacks p= placement-id support and X=/Y= subcell offset support; (6) tests assert substrings of the current malformed output instead of the protocol grammar. This epic tracks getting kittui to a verified spec-conformant baseline plus full ratakittui widget coverage proof. Child beads will land incrementally with grammar-pinned tests and a kittui proof CLI mode the operator can paste into Ghostty/kitty for visual confirmation.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

Acceptance: launch shell in a PTY, render terminal contents into kittui frames, resize with pane geometry, route typed input, and prove with an echo hello round trip. Parent epic: bd-031e54; directly helps bd-a9ec5b when xterm/XQuartz are unavailable. Filed from bd-a9ec5b session.

Lands the XServer trait implementation now; the CGVirtualDisplayCreate FFI itself behind unsafe extern "C" symbols, with a graceful fallback to capturing the main display when virtual display creation fails. title: Fix animation upload to use a=t/a=f frame appends and a=a control
description:
Animation upload currently uses repeated a=t,r=N which the spec does not document for appending frames. Per https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol/#animation the first frame uses a=t with f= and i= as usual, subsequent frames use a=f with the same i= to append, and the animation control sets gap/loop count via a=a with parameters s=, c=, z=. Audit kittui-kitty::upload_animation against the spec, fix the verb sequence, set per-frame delays via the appropriate field, add grammar tests, and ensure native looping plays without escape traffic per cycle.

title: kitwm: flicker between frames — drop \x1b[H\x1b[J + only re-emit changed windows
description: Operator: 'image flickers between frames. should be a smooth consistent WM experience, only insides of windows are latency bound but can't cause main WM to flicker'. Cause: session::run_loop writes \x1b[H\x1b[J every frame to clear, then re-emits each window's upload+placement+placeholder. The clear wipes the previous frame before the new pixels arrive over the slow tmux passthrough, so the operator sees a black flash per frame. Fix: stop clearing the screen, kitty atomically replaces an image at the same id; only emit placement+placeholder on first frame per window or when footprint changes; never re-emit when nothing changed; track last-seen window set so removed windows get a single delete escape. Parent: bd-031e54.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: WM-thread architecture: per-backend Pump (capture+encode), non-blocking UI tick
description: Move capture + PNG/raw-RGB encode off the UI thread. Each attached backend becomes a Pump with its own worker thread that loops capture -> encode -> store-in-slot. MultiCompositor::tick(layout) reads slots non-blockingly and returns whatever's there. UI thread keeps running input, resize, layout, hit-test at 60fps regardless of slow windows. Slow windows just show their previous frame until they catch up.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY socket: detach/reattach session daemon (graphical tmux)
description:
Split kittui-wm into a long-lived daemon process ("server") and a thin viewer process ("client"), communicating over a Unix domain socket at $KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY (e.g. /tmp/kittui-wm-0.sock, analogous to tmux's $TMUX or X11's $DISPLAY). The daemon owns all backends (Quartz capture, Xvfb, XQuartz, remote SSH), runs the Pump threads, holds the layout and focus state, and survives client disconnects. The client (kittui-wm-demo / kittui-wm attach) is just a renderer: connects to the socket, requests scene snapshots, sends pointer/key events back, redraws kitty graphics. Multiple clients can attach simultaneously to the same session (mirroring tmux's pane sharing) — useful for pair-programming-over-SSH and for SSH-survives-disconnect. Wire format: length-prefixed JSON or bincode; v1 supports CMD_LIST, CMD_TICK (returns last frame + window metadata), CMD_INPUT (forwards InputEvent), CMD_RESIZE, CMD_QUIT_SESSION. kittui-wm-demo grows --attach/--detach/--kill modes. Session ids are operator-configurable (KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY=:1 -> /tmp/kittui-wm-1.sock).\n\nMakes the whole stack tmux-shaped: daemon stays running, hosted apps survive client disconnect, you can SSH in from anywhere and reattach to your existing kittui-wm session at the exact state you left it, multiple displays render the same session. Parent epic: kittui-wm v2.

title: Encode kitty unicode-placeholder diacritics per spec
description:
placeholder_text in crates/kittui-kitty currently emits bare U+10EEEE characters with only a \x1b[38:2:r:g:b]m foreground color set to the low 24 bits of the image id. The kitty graphics spec (https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol/#unicode-placeholders) requires every placeholder cell to carry up to three combining diacritics encoding (row, column, most-significant-byte-of-image-id). Without these, kitty/Ghostty treat every cell as row 0 column 0 of the image and the placement does not anchor. Implement diacritic encoding from the spec's tables, support image ids whose top byte is non-zero, add exact-grammar unit tests, and snapshot the placeholder grid for a known fixture.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

Out of scope for v1: Wayland support, GPU passthrough, raw RGB(A) transmission, multi-display.

title: KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY socket: detach/reattach session daemon (graphical tmux)
description:
Split kittui-wm into a long-lived daemon process (server) and a thin viewer process (client), communicating over a Unix domain socket at KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY (e.g. /tmp/kittui-wm-0.sock, analogous to tmux TMUX or X11 DISPLAY). The daemon owns all backends (Quartz capture, Xvfb, XQuartz, remote SSH), runs the Pump threads, holds the layout and focus state, and survives client disconnects. The client (kittui-wm-demo / kittui-wm attach) is just a renderer: connects to the socket, requests scene snapshots, sends pointer/key events back, redraws kitty graphics. Multiple clients can attach simultaneously to the same session (mirroring tmux pane sharing) - useful for pair-programming-over-SSH and for SSH-survives-disconnect. Wire format: length-prefixed JSON or bincode; v1 supports CMD_LIST, CMD_TICK (returns last frame + window metadata), CMD_INPUT (forwards InputEvent), CMD_RESIZE, CMD_QUIT_SESSION. kittui-wm-demo grows --attach/--detach/--kill modes. Session ids are operator-configurable (KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY=:1 -> /tmp/kittui-wm-1.sock). creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md references-only mode for links images and footnotes

creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md exposes structured math metadata
description: Math expressions now render as placeholders, but MarkdownDocument and --metadata-json do not expose structured math data. Add inline/display math metadata, include it in metadata JSON and plain/rich metadata sections, and add tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Complete FFI ABI surface and generated header workflow
description: DESIGN.md defines a larger C ABI: runtime_configure, render_json, unplace, last_error, probe_json, bytes_free, abi_version_check, explicit byte lengths, cbindgen header snapshots, and ABI drift checks. Current kittui-ffi exposes runtime_new/free, place_json, string_free, abi_version, and a hand-maintained header/test scaffold. Implement the remaining ABI functions, preserve panic-safe boundaries, generate/update kittui.h through an xtask/cbindgen workflow, and add ABI snapshot validation.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev
spoken_name: complete ffi abi surface and header workflow

title: kittui-md metadata JSON includes source document metrics
description: Add source-level metrics to kittui-md --metadata-json, including byte length and line count, so downstream tools can correlate rendered metadata with input size. Keep schema_version stable and add tests for the metrics.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser binary: first-class native browser app
description: Operator direction: browser should be a first-class native kittwm app, not a demo subcommand. Provide a `kittwm-browser` binary (or equivalent) that uses the kittwm native app protocol to replace/spawn a browser pane, then drives headless Chrome for screenshots and input. Parent epic: bd-031e54; depends on native app protocol wiring.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui proof CLI command and Rust harness
description:
Add 'kittui proof' subcommand that, for each combination of (transport, animation on/off, unicode-placeholder vs absolute placement, single vs multi-placement, with/without subcell offset), prints labeled output of the exact escape bytes (hex + raw) and an optional --emit mode that prints them straight to the terminal so the operator can paste/run in Ghostty/kitty for visual verification. Mirror in Rust integration tests that snapshot the protocol bytes for each combination so 'cargo test -p kittui-cli proof' fails on any drift.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md exposes structured footnote metadata
description: Footnote references and definitions now render visibly, but MarkdownDocument and --metadata-json do not expose structured footnote data. Add footnote metadata for definitions, include it in JSON/plain/rich metadata where appropriate, and add tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm bench: measure capture pipeline (captures/s, p50/p95/p99, bytes/s)
description: Add 'kitwm bench [--seconds N] [--capture SPEC]' that runs raw_frames() in a tight loop for N seconds (default 3), records per-iteration latency, and prints: captures/s, mean/p50/p95/p99/max latency, total bytes captured, throughput MB/s. Supports --json. No raw mode, no rendering. Gives operators a real perf number for their host without spelunking /tmp/kittui-wm.log. Parent: bd-031e54.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm --pick-window: live Mac window picker that captures the chosen one
description: Add --pick-window flag to kitwm. On macOS with --features sck, lists CGWindowList::list_app_windows() in a kittui-rendered menu, lets the operator type to filter, arrows to navigate, Enter to surface that window as the capture target. Then runs the regular kitwm session against QuartzServer::with_target(Window(id)). Visible new capability per overnight loop directive. Parent: bd-ed1c33.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-xquartz: spawn XQuartz in nolisten mode, attach via x11rb
description:
New crate kittui-xquartz behind feature 'xquartz'. spawn() launches /opt/X11/bin/Xquartz -nolisten tcp -screen $DISPLAY 1024x768x24 -ac or attaches to an existing :N. Implements XServer trait by delegating to kittui-xvfb's x11rb code path (extract that into a shared helper if needed). Detects XQuartz install at /opt/X11/bin or /Library/X11. Returns Unavailable if not found. Parent epic: bd-031e54

New crate kittui-xvfb that owns an Xvfb subprocess (or attaches to an existing :N display), connects via XCB, lists toplevel windows, and captures their framebuffer regions via MIT-SHM. Emits per-window (id, rect, rgba_bytes) snapshots and damage rectangles. Test exercises the spawn + connect path under a Linux runner and gracefully skips on macOS.

Parent epic: bd-8bfc05

title: Add batch/many render entrypoints that share resolved config and runtime state
description: Operator direction: common usage should support setting config/env once, then calling kittui many times or in a batch in that scope. Add well-documented batch entrypoints that resolve config once, reuse Runtime/cache/probe state, render/place many scenes efficiently, and expose this through CLI (e.g. JSONL or manifest input), Rust library, and FFI/TS binding surfaces. Ensure per-item overrides remain possible and tests verify repeated calls do not re-probe/re-upload unnecessarily.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md stats mode summarizes document counts

creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md components-json inspection mode

creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm --attach -c CMD: one-shot daemon command for scripting
description: Add -c/--command flag to 'kitwm --attach' so operators can fire a single daemon command and read the reply without entering the interactive REPL. Useful for scripting: 'kitwm --attach -c WINDOWS | jq...'. Exit code maps to daemon ERR/OK. Parent: bd-031e54 (extends bd-5b8dd6).
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md mode categories catalog
description: Add --mode-categories and --mode-categories-json no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can list supported mode categories and counts before filtering by category. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Support placement_id (p=) and subcell offset (X=, Y=) on placement_command
description:
placement_command currently emits a=p,U=1,i=,c=,r= with no placement-id and no subcell pixel offset. The spec allows multiple placements of the same image via p= and subcell origin via X=/Y= for fractional positioning. Extend placement_command (or add a new builder) to accept placement_id and pixel offsets; thread through Runtime::place; expose via CLI.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Replace CGDisplayCreateImage with ScreenCaptureKit on macOS 12.3+
description: QuartzServer::capture currently uses CGDisplayCreateImage, which is soft-deprecated on macOS 14 and returns a black/dimmed frame on macOS 14/15/26 even when Screen Recording is granted. Operator reported black screen + ~1 FPS on macOS 26.4. Replace with ScreenCaptureKit (SCStream + SCStreamConfiguration) running on its own thread with latest-frame-wins, falling back to the legacy path only on macOS < 12.3. Add a non-black-frame assertion to the round-trip test. Affects both whole-display and per-window capture paths in CaptureTarget.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Expand kittui-wm from placeholder substrate to split/stack/tab layout semantics
description: DESIGN.md says kittui-wm currently reserves WindowTree/WindowGeometry but full split/stack/tab semantics and per-window scene generation should arrive after tmux showcase exercises diff-driven composition. Current crate description/source mark kittui-wm as a placeholder scaffold. Implement basic split, stack, and tab layout models, z-order handling, conversion to Composition entries, and tests for deterministic geometry output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Window picker: floating panel + Tab cycle to surface a Mac/X window

Inside the running kittui-wm session, hotkey (default Mod+Space) opens a kittui floating panel listing every window from QuartzServer::list_app_windows() + every X11 window across attached backends. Arrow keys navigate, Enter surfaces the window (calls QuartzServer::with_target(Window(id)) and attaches as a new backend, or pins an existing one). Mod+W cycles surfaced windows. Parent epic: bd-031e54

title: relative placement (P=,Q=,H=,V=) not implemented
description: Kitty placement grammar supports anchoring relative to another placement via P=<src image id>, Q=<src placement id>, H=<col offset>, V=<row offset>. PlacementOptions has none of these fields, so 'relative placement' is just missing. File as separate work since it's additive grammar + new tests. Parent: bd-42d7c1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm --serve + --status: minimal Unix-socket daemon protocol (PING/STATUS)
description: kitwm --serve binds a Unix socket at /tmp/kitwm-$USER.sock (override via KITWM_SOCK env), spawns an accept thread, and answers single-line text requests: 'PING' -> 'PONG'; 'STATUS' -> 'pid=N uptime=Xs sock=...'. kitwm --status connects to that socket, sends 'STATUS', prints the reply, exits non-zero if no daemon. Lays the protocol foundation for bd-aaea73 attach client without committing to a wire format yet. RAII Drop on the server unlinks the socket. Parent: bd-031e54 (decomposed from bd-fb5d9d).
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md snippets alias for code blocks
description: Add --snippets as a friendly alias for kittui-md --code-blocks so users can extract Markdown code snippets with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md table glyph atlas: render box drawing as connected image cells
description: Next proposal: complete the table glyph layout by generating a real cell-sized box-drawing glyph atlas and rendering markdown tables through image cells anchored with relative placement. Include a z-ordered background image/panel below tables. Builds on bd-aa77c5/bd-04718f.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui UI component + markdown rendering layer
description:
Operator request: kittui has boxes, gradients, strips, etc., but needs a first-class UI component layer and a markdown renderer built on it. Scope includes textbox, headings (h1/h2/h3), title/banner/header/footer, text chips, link chips, tables, and markdown-to-kittui rendering. The renderer should use kitty virtual placement plus relative non-virtual positioning anchors where appropriate so composed UI pieces can share connected layout/z-space semantics. Parent/area: kittui renderer + kittwm operator UI.

Operator clarification: this epic should produce a full kittui Markdown viewer, not just a library renderer. The viewer should run as a normal terminal app for rich kitty-graphics Markdown rendering, and also work as a kittwm-native app when launched inside kittwm context.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Replace substring-based protocol tests with exact-grammar regressions
description:
kittui-kitty unit tests assert .contains(...) on small substrings, which passed the previous malformed leading-comma upload chunks for months. Convert each public function to use exact-grammar regression tests (golden strings for fixed inputs, structural parsers for the rest) covering: upload still single chunk, upload still multi-chunk, upload animation 3 frames + control, placement direct + tmux + unicode, placeholder grid with diacritics for a 4x3 footprint and an image id with non-zero top byte, delete by id, delete by placement-id. Each test must also fail if a stray comma or missing q= regresses.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev
spoken_name: replace substring tests with grammar regressions

title: kitwm daemon APPS verb: expose launcher candidates through --attach
description: Bridge the launcher candidate source into the daemon protocol. Add APPS and APPS_JSON daemon verbs so 'kitwm --attach -c APPS' lists default launcher + candidates and 'kitwm --attach -c APPS_JSON' returns the same machine-readable structure as 'kitwm apps --json'. This makes the running daemon expose launcher data to clients before the floating menu lands. Parent: bd-031e54; extends bd-7e7f3d/bd-13d80f.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm daemon: socket server + KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY
description:
New binary kittui-wm-daemon (or kittui-wm serve subcommand) that listens on a Unix socket at $KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY (e.g. /tmp/kittui-wm-0.sock). Owns all backends + Pumps. Wire format: length-prefixed bincode. v1 messages: CmdListWindows, CmdTick, CmdInput(InputEvent), CmdResize { cols, rows }, CmdQuit. Daemon survives client disconnects. Parent epic: bd-031e54

title: kitwm doctor: one-shot diagnostics report (backends, displays, terminal, log, version)
description: Add 'kitwm doctor' subcommand that prints a self-contained health report: OS, binary version, available backends (sck/quartz/xvfb feature flags), display enumeration via Quartz (count + bounds), terminal probe (kitty graphics support, TERM, COLORTERM), /tmp/kittui-wm.log presence & last-modified, screen-recording permission hint. Output as plain text + JSON via --json. Useful for bug reports and quickly confirming a fresh checkout has the right toolchain. Parent: bd-031e54.

creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm: raw RGB transmission (kitty f=32) instead of PNG re-encode per frame
description: kittui_wm_demo currently spends most of each frame PNG-encoding a fresh capture. The kitty graphics spec supports raw RGB(A) transmission via f=24 (RGB) and f=32 (RGBA), which skips PNG entirely. Wire that through kittui-kitty's upload_still + upload_still_ex (already accepts UploadMedium::Direct{bytes}; just needs a CaptureFormat option and a code-path in Runtime::place to skip PNG). Should drop the demo's per-frame cost from ~60ms to ~10ms on a 320x192 capture.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev
